regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-07-16] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.23% YoY | core CPI 2.25% | core PCE 2.89% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.2%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.58% | 10Y-2Y 0.42% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 16.5

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ (research only; no execution path via CME futures, OANDA is forex-only). Scorecard caveat first: n=13 direction calls, hit rate 0.769, regime accuracy 0.267 — this is CALIBRATING. Treat everything below as intelligence, not a validated edge. Regime accuracy in particular is near coin-flip, so the regime label is an INPUT, not a signal.

1) MOVE-BY-MOVE READ. Both indices are net soft on the day: ES -0.31%, NQ -0.95%. NQ is the clear underperformer — it printed a prior-day range of ~29397-30062 and is now sitting near the LOW of that range (last 29412), while ES is holding much better inside its prior-day range (7571.75-7626.25). The likely catalyst mix: renewed Iran strikes and a revived Gulf conflict (multiple GEOPOLITICS headlines) plus rising oil/FTSE mining weakness is a classic risk-off tilt that hits high-beta tech (NQ) harder than the broader S&P (ES). There's a CPI-tagged crypto-up headline and a 'futures tick up on earnings' liveblog that partially cushion the tape, so the news is MIXED, not cleanly bearish. RSI confirms the split: ES RSI14 65.6 (still constructive/near-strong), NQ RSI14 47.1 (neutral-to-soft). Both remain well above their 200-day (ES +8.3%, NQ +11.2%) — the primary uptrend is intact; this is a pullback within an uptrend, not a trend break.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ (WHERE volume traded — NOT order-flow delta; I cannot see buy/sell aggression). ES: POC 7603.32, value area 7563.08-7615.15, dense high-volume nodes clustered 7589-7603. Price (7591.5) is trading BELOW its POC and below session VWAP (7606.33) — mild rejection of the value-area high but still inside value. Line in the sand for ES: value-area low 7563 — losing that opens the prior-week-low zone. NQ: POC 29693.93, value area 29476.65-29856.88, HVNs stacked 29585-29694. NQ (29412) has fallen BELOW its entire value area and below VWAP (29584.54) — it is trading in a low-volume shelf beneath accepted value, right at the prior-day low (29397). That is the tell: NQ is the weak leg, probing the edge of last week's structure (PWL 29303.5).

3) LEAD-LAG / REGIME. Contemporaneous corr 0.886 (high) — ES & NQ are moving together, which the system flags as BREADTH: broad agreement, more durable direction, 'trust the direction more.' BUT note the divergence flag is TRUE and the cumulative NQ/ES spread is negative (-2.76%), with NQ underperforming across 1d/5d/20d windows. Best lag corr is only -0.123 and leader is NONE — there is no clean lead-lag edge here. So: high correlation but persistent NQ underperformance = agreement on a mild DOWN/consolidation drift, not a clean trend to press. Combined with my own poor regime-accuracy track record (0.267), I do NOT lean hard on the BREADTH 'trust direction' instruction. Size accordingly: this is a lower-conviction environment.

4) EVENT CALENDAR. Nothing today forces a de-risk, but the window is closing in: FOMC 2026-07-29 (~13d), PCE ~07-31 (~15d), ISM Manufacturing ~08-03 (~18d). Dates for PCE/ISM are cadence estimates, not verified — FOMC is a maintained seed, verify vs Fed calendar. Implication: still >2 weeks out, so no event-driven size cut required today, but any swing exposure should anticipate widening stops / reducing size into the last week of July.

5) SYNTHESIS. Meta-regime: intact primary uptrend (both far above 200SMA) undergoing a geopolitics-driven, tech-led pullback. The tape is genuinely MIXED — geopolitics is risk-off, but earnings/CPI headlines are supportive, and the lead-lag shows no clean leader. NQ is technically the weaker instrument (below value area, below VWAP, at prior-day low), while ES is holding value better. Because the news is not clean and the lead-lag is not clean, I keep confidence LOW and the bias NEUTRAL with a slight downside/consolidation tilt on NQ specifically. Narrowed read on NQ: watch the prior-day low / value-area boundary at ~29397-29477. A decisive hold and reclaim of VWAP (29584) argues the pullback is done and favors a rotation back toward POC 29694; a clean break and acceptance below 29397 opens PWL 29303 and shifts the intraday tilt bearish. NEUTRAL-with-downside-watch, ~42% conviction, invalidation of the near-term soft bias on a reclaim ABOVE 29584 (VWAP); downside continuation confirmed only below 29397. This is intelligence, not an edge — trade small or stand aside until either the value-area reclaim or the value-area break resolves.
